home.installHint: Add ChimeLine to your home screen for quick access
home.installHintIOS: Tap the Share button, then "Add to Home Screen"

generator.title: QR Code Generator
generator.subtitle: Generate printable QR cards from a Spotify playlist
generator.playlistHint: Supports: spotify:playlist:ID, open.spotify.com URL, or playlist ID. The playlist must be owned by you or followed in your Spotify account — editorial and third-party playlists you don't follow cannot be fetched.
generator.seriesHint: Printed in the corner of each title card to identify the series.

setup.instruction1: Click the button to open Spotify.
setup.instruction2: Listen for an iconic theme—verify you hear sound.
setup.instruction3: Return to the app when ready. ✨

funFacts.1: When you press pause, you're actually playing John Cage's 4'33'' — a piece of music consisting entirely of silence.
funFacts.2: QR codes can store up to 3KB of data. ChimeLine uses about 40 bytes.
funFacts.3: The oldest song with a known composer is a Sumerian hymn from around 1400 BC.
funFacts.4: Vinyl records spin at 33⅓ RPM — a speed chosen in 1948 to fit a full symphony on one side.
funFacts.5: The first music video ever broadcast on MTV was 'Video Killed the Radio Star' by The Buggles, on August 1, 1981.
funFacts.6: A standard CD holds 74 minutes of audio — chosen specifically to fit Beethoven's 9th Symphony.
funFacts.7: Vangelis composed the Chariots of Fire soundtrack on a synthesizer — in 1981, that was controversial. The Academy gave him the Oscar for Best Original Score anyway.
funFacts.8: The Chariots of Fire theme was composed overnight by Vangelis, reportedly in a single take.
funFacts.9: Vangelis's full name was Evangelos Odysseas Papathanassiou — 'Vangelis' is just easier to fit on a poster.
funFacts.10: The Chariots of Fire film is set during the 1924 Paris Olympics, but the iconic beach running scene was filmed in St Andrews, Scotland.
